topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management and taxation of oil and gas extraction in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands shall apply to all oil removed under the lease.
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: U.S. Tax Policy and Job Creation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to U.S. tax policy aimed at promoting job creation and reducing outsourcing.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to develop and publish a list of countries that are tax havens for corporations.
Grants a preference in the award of federal contracts to contractors who have not engaged in outsourcing.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the suspension of the taxable income limitation on percentage depletion for oil and natural gas produced from marginal properties.
Document 3: Reduces for 2008 income tax purposes the taxable income of a taxpayer by $125,000.
Reduces the rate of tax on taxable income in excess of $125,000 by 5%.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase from $50,000 to $320,000 the amount of employer-provided group term life insurance that an employee can exclude from gross income.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make the tax credit for adoption expenses refundable.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the amount of distilled spirits excise tax revenue returned to the treasuries of Puerto Rico or the Virgin Islands.
Defines 'unreasonable and excessive rum subsidy' as the excess of government assistance paid to producers of distilled spirits.
Document 7: Grants business taxpayers a tax credit for up to 20% of insourcing expenses.
Denies a tax deduction for outsourcing expenses incurred in relocating a U.S. business outside the United States.

topic.name: Education Policy and Funding
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving education through funding, teacher retention, and resource allocation.

Document 1: Nanotechnology Education Act requires grants for nanotechnology education in schools.
Eligible grantees include various educational institutions.
Document 2: Keep Teachers Teaching Act amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to support teacher retention programs.
Includes professional development and mentoring for teachers.
Document 3: SKILLs Act authorizes funding for improving literacy through school libraries.
Requires qualified library media specialists in schools receiving improvement funds.
Document 4: Student Loan Fair Prepayment Act amends student loan repayment processes.
Focuses on borrower protections and loan management.
Document 5: A PLUS Act allows states to consolidate federal education funding.
Requires states to ensure fiscal control and educational opportunities for disadvantaged students.
Document 6: Tyler Clementi Higher Education Anti-Harassment Act mandates anti-harassment policies in higher education.
Authorizes grants for programs aimed at preventing harassment.
Document 7: SKILLs Act emphasizes the importance of school libraries in literacy education.
Expands grant programs for media resources and professional development.

topic.name: Medicare Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislation aimed at modifying and improving various aspects of the Medicare program.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to allow any Medicare beneficiary to enter into a contract with a non-participating physician.
Requires a Medicare beneficiary to agree in writing in such a contract to pay the physician.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to apply Medicare competitive bidding to vacuum erection systems.
Require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to phase-in a national mail order program for such devices.
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the 190-day lifetime limit on inpatient psychiatric hospital services.
Document 4: Amends title XI of the Social Security Act (SSA) with respect to the authority of the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to exclude from federal health programs certain individuals.

Establishes criminal penalties for anyone who knowingly and with intent to defraud purchases, sells, or distributes, or arranges for the purchase, sale, or distribution of two or more beneficiary identification or provider numbers.
Document 5: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make payment adjustments for cases identified as meeting or exceeding the fixed high episode efficient case threshold.
Directs the Secretary to establish separate thresholds for high episode efficient cases and episode outlier cases for LTC hospitals.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide for payments of direct graduate education costs under title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act in accordance with an alternative payment methodology for eligible hospitals.
Establishes a program to educate residents displaced from a graduate medical education (GME) program impacted by such disaster.
Document 7: This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to maintain calendar year 2015 Medicare reimbursement rates for colonoscopy procedures.
Medicare beneficiary cost-sharing is eliminated for colorectal cancer screening tests.

topic.name: Suspension of Tariffs on Chemicals and Materials
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various chemical products and manufacturing materials.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain plastic laminate sheets.
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Propenoic acid, polymer with diethenylbenzene.
Document 3: extend the suspension of duty on certain ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymers.
Document 4: extend the temporary suspension of duty on mixtures containing n-butyl-1,2-benzisothiazolin-3-one.
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on acrylic or modacrylic synthetic filament tow.
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain manufacturing equipment.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-ethylhexyl (4-chloro-2-methylphenoxy) acetate.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving health care, benefits, and support services for veterans and their families.

Document 1: Eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility to veterans who are active participants in the VA's health care system.
Requires the veteran to be an enrollee in the VA's patient enrollment system.
Document 2: Allows grants made by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) for homeless veterans' comprehensive services programs.
Revises eligibility under the grant program for entities serving homeless veterans with special needs.
Document 3: Entitles an eligible employee to up to 26 workweeks of leave during a 12-month period to care for a covered service member.
Defines covered service member as a member of the U.S. Armed Forces.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse family caregivers of disabled veterans for travel expenses.
Includes lodging and subsistence in connection with authorized VA treatment.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to award a decoration called the Department of Veterans Affairs Medal for Distinguished Public Service.
Allows the Secretary to award such medal posthumously to such a veteran who is deceased.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to make available to veterans specified information about educational institutions and programs of education.
Prohibits the approval of a course of education if the institution requires a student to waive the right to legal recourse.
Document 7: Extends from 12 to 15 years after discharge the authorized period for veterans with service-connected disabilities to enroll in certain VA vocational training.
Affects rehabilitation programs.

topic.name: Child Welfare and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving child welfare, protection, and support systems for vulnerable children and families.

Document 1: Amends part B of title IV of the Social Security Act to revise requirements for foster care and adoption assistance
Increased payment rate to states for short-term training of certain child welfare agency staff
Document 2: Requires certain employers to permit each employee to earn at least 1 hour of paid sick time for every 30 hours worked
Document 3: Eliminates the requirement that a child would have been eligible for aid under the former Aid to Families with Dependent Children program
Expresses the sense of the Congress that the AFDC eligibility requirement should be eliminated
Document 4: Directs the Attorney General to establish and maintain a database solely for collecting DNA information with respect to violent predators against children
Defines a 'violent predator against children' as a person who commits a crime of violence against an individual under age of 18
Document 5: Includes among the required uses of the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to operate a cyber tipline for reporting Internet-related child sexual exploitation
Document 6: Amends the Department of Education Organization Act to require the Department of Education to make publicly available investigations and sanctions related to campus sexual violence
Document 7: Amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to authorize research on child abuse or neglect involving cross-jurisdictional complications
Requires each state receiving a grant to include in its annual report the number of children reported as abused or neglected

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at protecting consumers in financial transactions, particularly in mortgage and credit-related matters.

Document 1: Requires each servicer of a home mortgage to provide in writing to a mortgagor specified prompt notifications and decisions regarding a written request for a short sale.
An aggrieved individual may bring a civil action for equitable relief and a monetary award of $1,000 for any violation of this Act.
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to study any bank holding company having more than $500 billion in consolidated assets with respect to favorable pricing of its debt.
Defines 'economic benefit' as the difference between actual loan terms offered and a reasonable estimate of what such terms might have been.
Document 3: Prohibits a third party online hotel reservation seller from charging a consumer's credit card unless the seller discloses all material terms of the transaction.
States may bring actions in federal courts to obtain damages, restitution, compensation, or other relief for ROSCA violations.
Document 4: Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require the creditor or lessor to provide to each obligor involved in a consumer credit transaction all disclosures required under such Act.
Document 5: Requires each servicer of a home mortgage to provide in writing to a mortgagor specified prompt notifications and decisions regarding a written request for a short sale.
An aggrieved individual may bring a civil action for equitable relief and a monetary award of $1,000 for any violation of this Act.
Document 6: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to raise the examination threshold that brings an insured depository institution within its supervisory purview from assets of $10 billion to $50 billion.
Increases the size of an insured depository institution subject to the Act's reporting requirements.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Act to increase limitations on the maximum original principal obligation of mortgages that may be purchased by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Gives the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency discretion to increase existing mortgage purchase limitations where warranted by higher median home prices.

topic.name: Tribal Land Management and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, recognition, and rights of tribal lands and communities in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the local communities in South Dakota...
requires the recipients of the parcels of the real property conveyed under this Act to bear the cost of the survey for their particular parcel.
Document 2: Designates specified federal land within Olympic National Park...
Includes those lands in the Quileute Indian Reservation.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Directs the Secretary to acquire, for the benefit of the Tribe, trust title to 200 acres of land.
Document 4: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to provide that any enterprise or institution owned and operated by an Indian tribe...is not considered an employer.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary of the Interior...to take it into trust for the benefit of the Tribe.
Designates Yuma county, Arizona, as the Tribe's service area for the delivery of federal services.
Document 6: Prohibits the Secretary of Energy...from using the power of eminent domain...unless they have received explicit permission...from each Indian tribe whose land would be affected.
Document 7: Directs the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture...to allow the state or tribe to assume all management and protection functions...

topic.name: Campaign Finance Transparency
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at increasing transparency and regulation in campaign financing and political communications.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution
applies this requirement to any political committee regarding cumulative contributions
Document 3: requires each candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission a copy of the candidate's income tax returns
tax return furnished to the FEC by a candidate shall be made publicly available
Document 4: Directs the Federal Trade Commission to revise the do-not-call registry provisions
prohibit politically-oriented recorded message telephone calls to telephone numbers listed on that registry
Document 6: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to treat as a campaign contribution any payment made for a coordinated expenditure
prescribes penalties for knowing and willful violation of this Act

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to immigration policies and reforms in the United States, addressing issues such as visa categories, border security, and the status of undocumented immigrants.

Document 1: H-2A nonimmigrant visa category
housing regulations for workers engaged in the range production of livestock.
Document 2: alien smuggling and related offenses
employee verification;
border and law enforcement;
Document 3: extend the EB-5 Regional Center program
extend the E-Verify program
Document 4: identify incarcerated criminal aliens
remove such aliens from the United States upon release.
Document 5: adjust to conditional nonimmigrant status
waive specified grounds of inadmissibility
Document 6: immigrant visa was issued for a child who has been lawfully adopted
visa fees relating to a renewal or replacement may be waived
Document 7: border security and enforcement provisions
protection for asylum seekers and other immigrants

topic.name: Military Operations and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the operations, support, and welfare of military personnel and associated defense activities.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Defense, upon request of a member of the Armed Forces receiving outpatient care at or through Walter Reed Army Medical Center, to transfer such member to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs for the receipt of such care.
Requires such Secretaries to jointly provide such members information on the nature and quality of outpatient care services available through the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury, during a federal funding gap impacting the Armed Forces, to transfer to the Secretary of Defense amounts necessary to continue to provide pay and allowances to members of the Armed Forces.
Prohibits any such transfer after December 31, 2011.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2007 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of the Department of Defense.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2007, for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to develop and promulgate guidelines for the Department of Defense to have supplies made in factories or arsenals owned by the United States.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress on Army arsenal capabilities, workload, performance, and required capital investments.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Defense to submit to Congress a plan to enhance security of military personnel at DOD installations in the United States.
Implement the plan recommendations within 90 days after enactment of this Act.
Document 6: Directs the President to develop and transmit to the appropriate congressional committees a comprehensive interagency strategy and implementation plan for long-term security and stability in Pakistan.
Authorizes appropriations for Pakistan and the Pakistan Counterinsurgency Capability Fund.
Document 7: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program.

topic.name: Food and Drug Labeling Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the labeling and regulation of food and drugs to ensure safety and transparency for consumers.

Document 1: Trans Fat Truth in Labeling Act of 2009 requires labeling of trans fat content.
Directs the FDA to revise federal nutrition labeling regulations.
Document 2: Stop Oxy Abuse Act of 2010 modifies approval and labeling of controlled-release oxycodone.
Limits approval of drugs containing controlled-release oxycodone for severe-only pain.
Document 3: Carl's Law requires opioid drugs to state addiction risk prominently on labels.
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act regarding opioid labeling.
Document 4: Biotechnology Food Labeling Uniformity Act mandates labeling of genetically engineered food.
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act for GMO ingredient disclosure.
Document 5: Deems genetically-engineered fish unsafe under food safety provisions.
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to ensure safety in food labeling.
Document 6: PACT Act prohibits sale of dextromethorphan to individuals under 18 without supervision.
Imposes penalties for violations related to dextromethorphan distribution.
Document 7: FDA Amendments Act includes postmarket safety activities in drug application review.
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to enhance drug safety measures.

topic.name: Veterans Mental Health Services
topic.description: Legislation and programs aimed at improving mental health care and support services for veterans, including substance use disorders and PTSD.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure the provision of specified substance use disorder treatment services
Requires a program of enhanced care and treatment for veterans with substance use disorders and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
Document 2: Requires the Department of Defense (DOD) and the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to jointly commence a pilot program
to meet the increasing demand for mental health care providers at such facilities
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to initiate and sustain mental health first aid training programs
training on mental health resources, including the location of community mental health centers
Document 4: to improve mental and behavioral health services and outreach on college and university campuses
target underserved and at-risk populations
Document 5: to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
for programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems
Document 6: to develop, implement, or enhance veteran's treatment courts
for programs that involve continuing judicial supervision over nonviolent offenders with substance abuse or mental health problems
Document 7: to provide specified services to individuals who are suffering from serious mental illness
are homeless or at imminent risk of becoming homeless

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on various aspects of agricultural policy, including support for farmers, rural development, and food programs.

Document 7: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to require the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to reduce federal crop insurance payments.
The bill establishes an annual cap on total reimbursements for administrative and operating costs of crop insurance providers.

topic.name: U.S. Sanctions and Support Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to sanctions against Iran and support for Ukraine, focusing on foreign policy, national security, and economic assistance.

Document 1: Sets forth U.S. policy regarding Ukraine, including support for the sovereignty and territorial integrity of a democratic Ukraine.
Authorizes and encourages the President to provide assistance to support democracy and civil society in Ukraine.
Document 2: This bill amends the Iran Threat Reduction and Syrian Human Rights Act of 2012 to require the President to block and prohibit all transactions in property and property interests of a foreign person that knowingly supports or conducts certain transactions with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps.
The President shall review an identified foreign person to determine whether there is sufficient evidence to impose sanctions.
Document 3: Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to direct the President to impose two or more sanctions under such Act if a person has, with actual knowledge, made an investment of $20 million or more that directly and significantly contributed to Iran's ability to develop its petroleum resources.
Directs the President to report to the appropriate congressional committees every six months on such activities.
Document 4: Expresses the sense of Congress that Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) meets the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization.
The Secretary shall report to Congress on whether the IRGC meets such criteria.

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on social security benefits, employment opportunities for individuals with disabilities, and tax provisions related to senior citizens and church plans.

Document 1: Medicaid Buy-In State meeting certain criteria may receive bonus payments for expanding individual integrated employment and reducing subminimum wage work.
Actions conducive to expanding employment opportunities for individuals with disabilities.
Document 2: Economic recovery payments to recipients of Social Security, Supplemental Security Income (SSI), and veterans disability compensation.
Extends special tax credit for certain government retirees.
Document 3: Amends Internal Revenue Code pension plan provisions related to church plans.
Allows tax-free transfers to and mergers of church plans.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to issue U.S. obligations equal to monthly insurance benefits payable under OASDI.
Exempts such obligations from the public debt limit.
Document 5: Allows an employee to elect irrevocably to have employment treated as covered for Social Security benefit purposes.
Employer subject to Social Security taxes.
Document 6: Requires notification when Social Security account number does not match relevant records.
Notification of fraudulent use of Social Security account number.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to make available a new federal income tax Form 1040S for individuals over 65.
Allows use of the form even if income includes social security benefits.

topic.name: Aviation Regulations and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses various regulations and initiatives aimed at enhancing safety, training, and operational standards within the aviation industry.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to issue regulations that require air carriers to provide initial and annual recurring training for flight attendants and gate attendants
recognizing intoxicated passengers, and dealing with disruptive passengers.
Document 3: Directs the Office of the Ombudsman of the Transportation Security Administration (TSA) to record complaints from the public regarding TSA screening practices
assist in training TSA agents regarding such practices.
Document 4: Authorizes appropriations for civil aviation research and development
develop technologies and methods to assess the risk of and prevent defects, failures, and malfunctions in all classes of drones.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Coral Reef Conservation Act Reauthorization and Enhancement Amendments of 2016
NOAA must also submit an international coral reef ecosystem strategy to Congress
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
Authorizes funds for monitoring and assessment, research, pollution reduction, education, and technical support
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
Defines 'coral reef ecosystem' as the system of coral reefs and geographically associated species, habitats, and environment

topic.name: Labor and Employment Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating labor relations, employment discrimination, and workplace rights.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require attestation and proof of citizenship or lawful residency from employees seeking labor representation.
Collective bargaining.
Document 2: Changes the effective date of amendments made by the Paul Wellstone and Pete Domenici Mental Health Parity and Addiction Equity Act of 2008.
Group health plans maintained pursuant to collective bargaining agreements.
Document 3: Authorizes an employee to request from an employer a change in the terms or conditions of the employee's employment.
Makes it unlawful for an employer to interfere with any rights provided to an employee under this Act.
Document 4: Prohibits employment discrimination on the basis of actual or perceived sexual orientation or gender identity.
Provides for enforcement, including giving the EEOC, the Librarian of Congress, the Attorney General, and U.S. courts the same enforcement powers.
Document 5: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from approving an employer petition for any visa authorizing employment in the United States unless the employer has certified to the Secretary of Labor.

Exempts an employer from such requirements if the employer certifies to the Secretary of Labor that the total number of the employer's workers who are U.S. citizens will not be reduced by a mass layoff.
Document 6: Amends the Fair Housing Act, with respect to prohibited discrimination in housing sales and rentals, to specify that the race, color, religion, sex, familial status, or national origin basis of discrimination may be actual or perceived.
Redefines 'discriminatory housing practice' to specify that the definition applies regardless of whether the discriminatory practices occur pre- or post-acquisition.
Document 7: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to publish detailed criteria used by the Secretary to approve a waiver, adjustment, or other compliance relief.
Prohibits any preferential treatment to be given to an applicant based on political contributions or association with a labor union.

topic.name: Communications Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating and improving communication services, including spectrum allocation, broadcasting rights, and support for public safety communications.

Document 1: Voluntary Incentive Auctions Act of 2010 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to provide that, if consistent with the public interest for a licensee to voluntarily relinquish some or all of its spectrum usage rights...
Prohibits the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) from reclaiming frequencies of broadcast television licensees or any other licensees on an involuntary basis for certain licensing purposes.
Document 2: Satellite Television Access Reauthorization Act of 2014 - Amends federal copyright law... to extend until December 31, 2019, the statutory license under which satellite carriers retransmit distant television broadcast stations...
Expands the local service area for cable retransmissions of low power television stations.
Document 3: Cell Tax Fairness Act of 2009 - Prohibits states or local governments from imposing any new discriminatory tax on mobile services...
Defines 'new discriminatory tax' as a tax imposed on mobile services, providers, or property that is not generally imposed on other types of services or property.
Document 4: Rural Broadband Investment Act of 2013 or the RBI Act of 2013 - Suspends regulations of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC)...
Requires the FCC to initiate a rulemaking to adopt a revised proposal for rural support...
Document 5: Broadband for First Responders Act of 2010 - Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to increase the electromagnetic spectrum allocation for public safety services...
Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to allocate the paired electromagnetic spectrum bands... for public safety broadband communications.
Document 6: Next Generation Television Marketplace Act of 2011 - Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 concerning...
Extends exemptions from copyright infringement laws to certain secondary transmissions by cable systems and satellite carriers.
Document 7: Wireless Innovation and Public Safety Act of 2011 - Increases the allocation of electromagnetic spectrum for use by public safety entities...
Authorizes the establishment of a private, nonprofit Public Safety Broadband Corporation to deploy a nationwide, interoperable public safety broadband network.

topic.name: Privacy and Data Protection Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing privacy rights and protecting sensitive personal information in electronic communications and data management.

Document 1: Prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging...
Requires the government to obtain a warrant from a court before requiring providers to disclose the content of such communications.
Document 2: Require federal agencies to make information that is disclosable under such Act available to the public in an electronic, publicly accessible format.
Establish a presumption of openness in administering FOIA by prohibiting an agency from withholding information otherwise disclosable under FOIA.
Document 3: Requires any commercial entity...to develop, implement, maintain, and enforce a written program, containing administrative, technical, and physical safeguards, for the security of sensitive personal information.
Requires a covered entity to report a security breach to the Federal Trade Commission (FTC).
Document 4: Prohibit a provider of remote computing service or electronic communication service to the public from knowingly divulging...
Requires a law enforcement agency...to provide the customer a copy of the warrant and a notice that such information was requested.
Document 5: Prohibits a person, other than the owner or lessee of the motor vehicle, from accessing data recorded or transmitted by such a recorder unless...
Prohibits...the disclosure of personally identifiable information of the vehicle owner or lessee.
Document 6: Requires DOJ to facilitate information sharing between the NamUs databases and the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) database.
DOJ must also promulgate rules that protect confidential, private, and law enforcement sensitive information.
Document 7: Directs the Attorney General to establish national interoperability standards to facilitate the exchange of prescription information...
Require states to ensure that databases established under the CS Program comply with such standards.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation and Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation, management, and protection of wildlife and natural habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Designates specified land and inland water within the Sleeping Bear Dunes National Lakeshore in Michigan as wilderness
Requires the boundary of the Wilderness to be 100 feet from the centerline of adjacent county roads
Document 2: Allows payments to a conservation banking program for military construction that may impact protected species
Engaged in military testing that may result in adverse impact to one or more species protected
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require a searchable database of federal expenditures for civil actions
Requires the Secretary to submit an annual report to Congress
Document 4: Amends the Lacey Act to prohibit the possession of prohibited wildlife species
Includes exemptions for accredited institutions and wildlife sanctuaries
Document 5: Amends the Toxic Substances Control Act to exclude certain hunting and fishing equipment from its definition
Authorizes states to pay for public target ranges
Document 6: Bars the Secretary of the Interior from using federal funding to purchase land resulting in a net increase of land acreage
Makes exceptions for certain land purchases
Document 7: Amends the Horse Protection Act to establish a new system for inspecting horses for soring
Prohibits causing a horse to become sore for the purpose of showing or auctioning

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing support and resources for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to allow the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to employ additional export finance specialists
Requires the Administrator to report to ...
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to extend through September 27, 2012 a temporary increase from $350,000 to $1 million in the maximum loan amount under the Express Loan Program
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2014-FY2018 for the SelectUSA Initiative
federal assistance aimed at increasing both foreign and domestic investment in U.S. business
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the procurement program for women-owned small businesses
Allows a contracting officer to award a sole source contract to any economically disadvantaged women-owned small business
Document 5: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish and carry out a program to provide support to SBA regional, district, and branch offices
Makes permanent the SBA's Community Express Program
Document 6: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a three-year pilot program to permit an eligible certified development company (CDC) to make loans
Removes the requirement that such a CDC maintain separate and distinct memberships, officers, boards of directors, and loan committees
Document 7: Directs the Securities and Exchange Commission to modify its regulations under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
Requires such regulations to provide that a non-accelerated filer does not have to provide management's report on internal control over financial reporting

topic.name: Land Use and Management Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to land use, management, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Directs the Bureau of Consumer Financial Protection (CFPB) to prescribe regulations to prohibit any lender from making any federally related mortgage loan under which a home inspection is conducted in connection with settlement of the mortgage loan unless the inspection is conducted by a home inspector licensed, certified, approved, or otherwise regulated by the inspector's home state.
Amends the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974 to direct the CFPB to include home inspection information in settlement forms.
Document 2: Directs the Department of the Interior to convey, without consideration, to SEND North in Anchorage, Alaska, the reversionary interests of the United States in and to a specified area of 210 acres located in the city of Glennallen.
Enabling its economic development.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to convey approximately five acres of certain identified National Forest System land in Santa Barbara County to the White Lotus Foundation.
Subjects the land exchange to provisions under the Federal Land Policy and Management Act of 1976.
Document 4: Designates certain areas in California as wilderness areas and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Amends the Wild and Scenic Rivers Act to designate segments of the following as components of the national wild and scenic rivers system.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey certain National Forest System land in the San Bernardino National Forest.
Requires the Secretary to relocate the portion of the Pacific Crest National Scenic Trail located on the federal land to adjacent NFS land.

topic.name: Voting and Election Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative measures aimed at regulating and improving the electoral process in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits U.S. government assistance to Egypt pursuant to the coup d'etat restriction under the Department of State, Foreign Operations, and Related Programs Act, 2012.
Document 2: Amends the Home Owners' Loan Act to prohibit any company or subsidiary from holding, soliciting, or exercising any proxies in respect of a mutual savings association.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any person conducting a federal election phone bank to report to the Federal Election Commission specified information.
Document 4: Amends the National Voter Registration Act of 1993 to require each state to make available official public websites for online voter registration.
Document 5: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to direct the Election Assistance Commission to develop and adopt guidelines for electronic poll books.
Document 6: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives.
Document 7: Establishes the Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac Investigative Commission to investigate and make recommendations to Congress regarding certain decisions of the Federal National Mortgage Association.

topic.name: U.S. Financial and Trade Regulations
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative acts aimed at regulating financial transactions, trade practices, and international relations, particularly concerning Cuba and customs duties.

Document 1: Amends federal money and finance law to repeal certain discretionary exemptions of depository institutions from currency transaction reporting requirements.
Defines qualified customer as any person that is incorporated or organized under federal or state law.
Document 2: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the Commissioner responsible for U.S. Customs and Border Protection to initiate investigations into claims of evasion of antidumping or countervailing duties.
Requires the posting of a cash deposit for each entry of covered merchandise.
Document 3: Prohibits the use of funds made available to the Department of the Treasury to implement regulations to require specific licenses for travel-related transactions in Cuba.
Document 4: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to determine an individual countervailable subsidy rate for an exporter or producer.
The bill shall apply to any investigation or review initiated by such authority on or after March 1, 2015.
Document 5: Directs the U.S. Trade Representative to report to Congress annually on the estimated annual loss of revenue to holders of U.S. intellectual property rights as a result of violations in China.
Requires the USTR to impose duties on merchandise from China equivalent to the estimated annual loss of revenue.
Document 6: Directs the Transportation Security Administration to report to Congress on security measures at Cuba's international airports.
Shall establish agreements with the Government of Cuba to allow the Federal Air Marshal Service to conduct missions on flights between the U.S. and Cuba.
Document 7: Prohibits the President from regulating or prohibiting travel to or from Cuba by U.S. citizens or legal residents.
States that such restrictions shall not apply if the United States is at war with Cuba.

topic.name: Support for Minority Communities
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing education, health, and economic development for minority and indigenous communities in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes within the National Science Foundation (NSF) an Office of Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology
to provide educational instruction in digital and wireless network technologies
Document 2: Amends the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act to permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages
to organize as Urban Corporations and to receive certain settlement land
Document 4: Requires any Department of Health and Human Services grant to or contract with Papa Ola Lokahi
to support community-based initiatives that reflect holistic approaches to health
Document 5: Amends the Indian Arts and Crafts Act of 1990 to authorize any federal law enforcement officer to conduct an investigation
of an alleged offense involving the sale of any good that is misrepresented as an Indian produced good
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to make miscellaneous changes to the Mentoring grant program
that include improvement of school connectedness and character education among mentoring's purposes
Document 7: Amends the Native American Business Development, Trade Promotion, and Tourism Act of 2000 to establish duties for the Office of Native American Business Development
including advising the Department of Commerce regarding the relationship between the United States and Indian tribes

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Policy
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures and policies related to abortion rights, restrictions, and funding in the United States.

Document 1: prohibits any government from imposing on abortion services
makes abortion services more difficult to access
Document 2: prohibit the use of tissue from an induced abortion for research
Soliciting or acquiring a donation of fetal tissue from an induced abortion for a purpose other than disposal is prohibited
Document 3: prohibit any person from performing or attempting to perform an abortion
prohibits the abortion from being performed if the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater
Document 4: authorize the President to make a voluntary contribution on a grant basis to the United Nations Population Fund
reduce abortion and related maternal mortality
Document 5: prohibits the availability of federal funds for any purpose to Planned Parenthood
restriction will not apply in cases of rape or incest
Document 6: prohibits the availability of federal funds for any purpose to Planned Parenthood
Department of Health and Human Services must seek repayment of federal assistance
Document 7: require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offers coverage of abortion
Preempts any state law requiring coverage of or funding for abortions

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, management, and financial obligations of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Prohibits bonuses or other rewards to the Postmaster General or any other officer of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) in a senior executive position in any year in which any postal retail facility or mail processing facility is closed.
Repeals exceptions to the limit on total compensation for USPS officers and employees.
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) to identify annually a list of postal properties with space available for use by federal agencies.
Establishes a Federal Property Council to develop guidance and ensure implementation of an efficient and effective property management strategy.
Document 3: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to issue a special postage stamp for first-class mail that costs not less than 15% more than the regular first-class stamp to contribute funding for breast cancer research.
Requires the National Institutes of Health (NIH) and the Department of Defense (DOD) to annually report to Congress on the use of any such funding.
Document 4: Directs the United States Postal Service to study and report to the appropriate congressional committees on the feasibility of using postal employees to serve as enumerators in the 2020 decennial census.
Document 5: Prescribes the 'average pay' and the appropriate percentage thereof to be used in determining annuities for civilian employment with the United States Postal Service (USPS).
Expresses the intent of Congress that this Act shall apply to allocations of past, present, and future benefit liabilities between the USPS and the Treasury.
Document 6: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act to give public water systems the option to mail the annual report on the level of contaminants in the drinking water or make such report available on the system's website.
Requires such report to include for any regulated contaminant in such water system for which there has been a violation.
Document 7: Amends the Safe Drinking Water Act by requiring the Environmental Protection Agency to revise regulations about consumer confidence reports.
Each community water system must mail their customers an annual consumer confidence report that provides information about local drinking water quality.

topic.name: Water and Mineral Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, reclamation, and sustainable use of water and mineral resources in the United States.

Document 1: authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the design, planning, and construction of permanent facilities needed to establish recycled water distribution
provide recycled water in that District.
Document 2: establishes the Abandoned Locatable Mine Reclamation Fund
requires each state and each Indian tribe that receives a grant to establish an abandoned mine reclamation fund.
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to construct, operate, and maintain the Northwestern New Mexico Rural Water Supply Project
establishes in the Treasury a Reclamation Water Settlements Fund.
Document 4: amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act to prohibit the Office of Surface Mining and Reclamation Enforcement from accepting new self-bonds for coal reclamation
requires the Department of Interior to issue rules establishing limitations on surety bonds.
Document 5: prohibit payments from the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund to a state or Indian tribe certified as having completed coal reclamation.
Document 6: transfers to the Secretary of Labor the duty to establish a schedule reducing the average concentration of respirable dust
requires a mine operator to deliver a complete copy of the examining physician's report to any miner.
Document 7: establishes in the Treasury the Abandoned Mine Reclamation Trust Fund
appropriates to such Fund amounts equal to 50% of the additional revenues received due to the disallowance of the depletion allowance.

topic.name: Veterans and Commemorative Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the recognition, preservation, and commemoration of veterans and historical events in the United States.

Document 1: Mount Soledad Veterans Memorial Preservation Act
convey the Mt. Soledad Veterans Memorial
Document 3: Peace Corps Commemorative Foundation
commemorate the formation of the Peace Corps
Document 7: Korean War Veterans Recognition Act of 2008
National Korean War Veterans Armistice Day

topic.name: Occupational Health and Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing safety and health protections for workers across various industries, including mining, transportation, and cosmetics.

Document 1: Amends the Occupational Safety and Health Act of 1970 (OSHA) to expand its coverage to federal, state, and local government employees.
Increases protections for whistle blowers under OSHA.
Document 2: Requires the NTSB to investigate derailments involving a significant release of hazardous materials into the environment.
DOT shall establish and enforce a national standard for the maximum volatility of crude oil.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Mine Safety and Health Act of 1977 to require the Secretary of Labor to conduct health and safety related accident investigations.
Requires an independent accident investigation by an independent panel appointed by the Secretary of Health and Human Services.
Document 4: Imposes enhanced criminal penalties for violations related to controlled substances.
Directs the United States Sentencing Commission to review its guidelines and policy statements to reflect the enhanced penalties.
Document 5: Requires a site operator to provide information in a clear and conspicuous way on its website regarding the health and safety risks of overseas vacation destinations.
Treats a violation as an unfair or deceptive act or practice prescribed under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
Document 6: Requires any establishment engaged in manufacturing and packaging cosmetics for use in the United States to register annually and pay a fee.
Requires the Secretary to establish labeling requirements and safety standards for cosmetics.
Document 7: Requires each operator of a gas pipeline facility to accelerate the repair, rehabilitation, and replacement of gas piping or equipment that is leaking.
Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration to issue non-binding best practices guidelines.

topic.name: Veteran and Emergency Support Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support, training, and resources to veterans and individuals affected by emergencies or disasters.

Document 1: expedited review of J-visa applications of aliens applying for U.S. admission to participate in a program under which they will receive graduate medical education or training.
Document 3: entitle an eligible veteran of the Armed Forces...pursuing a full-time educational program of apprenticeship or other on-job training to a monthly benefit payment
Document 4: temporary housing, medical, and humanitarian assistance to individuals and families dislocated due to an emergency or major disaster
Document 7: modifies the treatment of Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) agreements with service providers to furnish veterans with nursing home care, adult day health care, or other extended care services.

topic.name: Education and Bankruptcy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation addressing educational rights, student loan discharge in bankruptcy, and related educational programs for disadvantaged students.

Document 1: Amends the federal bankruptcy code to allow the discharge in bankruptcy of an educational loan
Currently, those debts are dischargeable only if excepting them from discharge would impose an undue hardship
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to make annual determinations as to whether each state's public school system provides all its students with the educational resources to succeed academically
Requires such education to enable students to acquire the knowledge and skills necessary for responsible citizenship
Document 5: Amends the Johnson-O'Malley Act to direct the Secretary of the Interior to enter into contracts with tribal organizations
Requires contract funds to be used for remedial instruction, counseling, and cultural programs

topic.name: Veterans and Military Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights, benefits, and protections for veterans, military personnel, and their families.

Document 1: National Guard and Reserve Retired Pay Equity Act of 2008
members of Ready Reserve components of the armed forces shall receive credit in determining eligibility for early receipt of non-regular service retired pay.
Document 2: Tomb of Remembrance for the interment of cremated fragments of the remains of members of the Armed Forces
interment of cremated fragments of the remains of members of the Armed Forces killed in Afghanistan, Iraq, or any subsequent war.
Document 4: Veterans Pensions Protection Act of 2010
excludes from annual income for purposes of eligibility for pensions for veterans and their surviving spouses and children.
Document 5: Veterans Identity Protection Act
to receive, process, and pay claims for injuries suffered as a result of the unauthorized use, disclosure, or dissemination of identifying information stolen from the Department of Veterans Affairs.
Document 6: certificate of service to Filipino nationals
military service in the Philippine Islands during World War II which qualifies such a person or a survivor to receive any military, veterans', or other benefits under U.S. laws.

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Safety and Welfare
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving public safety, health, and welfare across different sectors including education, consumer products, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Establishes in the legislative branch a National Homelessness Task Force
evaluate the effectiveness of federal programs addressing homelessness
Document 2: Prohibits an institution of higher education from receiving federal funds
national championship game is the final game of a single elimination post-season playoff system
Document 3: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990
establish a Federal Oil Spill Research Committee
Document 4: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Improvement Act of 2008
requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to issue a final rule on safety standards
Document 5: Reauthorizes appropriations for the grant program
training of school personnel to recognize child sexual abuse
Document 6: amends the Public Health Service Act
grants must be awarded to schools that serve students in grades 6-8
Document 7: Directs the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to determine with respect to standards
to initiate a rulemaking proceeding for a consumer product safety rule regarding standards for youth football helmets

topic.name: Climate Change and Marine Ecosystems
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to climate change impacts, marine ecosystem health, and the management of natural resources to address environmental challenges.

Document 1: prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: authorizes the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to declare and respond to a marine disease emergency
likely to threaten the sustainability of a marine species or the health of a marine ecosystem.
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Commerce to issue a loan to refinance the existing debt obligation funding the fishing capacity reduction program
for the West Coast groundfish fishery implemented under the Department of Commerce and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2003.
Document 4: requires the Director of National Intelligence to submit to Congress a National Intelligence Estimate on the anticipated geopolitical effects of global climate change
implications of such effects on U.S. national security.
Document 5: states as U.S. policy that federal land and waters should be managed for the benefit of the people of the United States to avoid the most dangerous impacts of climate change
to promote a rapid transition to a clean energy economy.
Document 6: establishes within the Department of State an Office on Global Climate Change
to advance U.S. goals with respect to reducing global greenhouse gas emissions.
Document 7: creates a grant program for the establishment of Indian Youth Service Corps
to carry out projects determined to be priorities by their tribes and communities.

topic.name: Federal Land and Resource Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, use, and conservation of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to prohibit the Administrator of the EPA from promulgating a revised or new water quality standard for a pollutant when the Administrator has approved a state water quality standard.
Prohibits the specification of any defined area as a disposal site for the discharge of dredged or fill material into navigable waters without state concurrence.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop recommendations for evaluation measures and criteria for programs under this Act.
Provides low-cost revolving loans and loan guarantees to eligible entities for the commercial application of energy or other technologies.
Document 3: Designates certain lands in the Skykomish River Valley, Washington, as the Wild Sky Wilderness, to be managed by the Secretary of Agriculture.
Authorizes the Secretary to acquire lands and interests in the Wild Sky Wilderness.
Document 4: Amends provisions concerning the Transition Assistance Program of the Department of Defense to include employment and job training assistance for members of the Armed Forces.
Requires the VA Secretary to submit to the congressional veterans committees the results of a study on veterans' benefits instruction.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Agriculture to offer for competitive sale by auction 8% of the federal lands managed by the Bureau of Land Management.
Requires the lands to be sold to be configured to maximize their marketability.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to the Alaska Railroad Corporation all rights, title, and interests of the United States to certain land for the purpose of constructing a railroad corridor extension.
Requires Surface Transportation Board approval for the phased construction of such extension.
Document 7: Amends title XVI of the Social Security Act to increase resource limits for aged, blind, or disabled individuals.
Sets forth income rules imputing income from certain deferred compensation arrangements.

topic.name: Terrorism and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This cluster discusses legislative measures aimed at enhancing financial regulation and information sharing in the context of terrorism prevention and response.

Document 1: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to repeal the contingent conditions under which the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers (NARAB) shall not be established.

Requires the NARAB to provide a mechanism for the adoption and multi-state application of requirements pertaining to licensing, continuing education, and other qualifications of non-NARAB insurance producers.
Document 3: Amends the USA PATRIOT Act to allow financial institutions to share information regarding individuals suspected of unlawful activities.
Expresses the need for federal law enforcement agencies and financial institutions to share information about terrorist activities.
Document 4: Allows financial assistance for terrorism prevention activities to be used to pay salaries of intelligence analysts.
Facilitates information and intelligence sharing activities.
Document 6: Amends the Terrorism Risk Insurance Act regarding the blocked assets of a terrorist party.
Redefines 'blocked asset' to include any asset seized or frozen by the United States under the Foreign Narcotics Kingpin Designation Act.
Document 7: Requires federal departments to provide information about persons under federal terrorism investigation.
Directs the Department of Justice to establish a process to delay firearm transfers to individuals under terrorism investigation.

topic.name: Animal and Environmental Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at protecting animal welfare, human health, and environmental resources.

Document 1: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to list permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities
Sets forth additional monetary penalties for related violations.
Document 2: Requires the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to inspect establishments that engage in the manufacture of human cells, tissues, and cellular and tissue-based products
Sets forth penalties for failing to comply with model form requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to reestablish the Program Office of the Gulf of Mexico Program
Authorize the EPA Administrator to provide grants for monitoring water quality and living resources.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to enter into a cooperative agreement with Wyoming to conduct certain forest and watershed restoration services
Includes treatment of insect-infected trees and the reduction of hazardous fuels.
Document 5: Authorizes the Department of the Interior to acquire by donation approximately 44 acres of land to include in the John Muir National Historic Site.
Document 6: Directs the President to establish an integrated, five-year Global HIV/AIDS-Free Generation Strategy
States that it is U.S. policy to ensure efforts to combat HIV/AIDS globally should expand and coordinate operations research.
Document 7: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act with respect to consumer credit protection requirements
Requires a consumer reporting agency to provide free disclosures to any consumer who has received a notice of adverse action.

topic.name: Health and Environmental Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts related to health care, environmental protection, and the regulation of pharmaceuticals.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to include activities for the remediation of sediment contamination.
Authorizes appropriations for remediation and public information programs.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress regarding health benefits obligations to retired military personnel.
Prohibits increases in premiums for DOD medical care coverage.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from claiming title to water absent specific direction of law.
Prevents impairment of water rights allocated under federal law.
Document 4: Treats independent pharmacies negotiating with health plans under antitrust laws similarly to employees.
Exempts good faith actions from criminal sanctions.
Document 5: Amends the McCarran-Ferguson Act to ensure antitrust laws apply to health insurance.
Prohibits unfair methods of competition in health insurance.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act regarding the definition of 'first applicant' for generic drugs.
Requires a substantially complete application for new drug filing.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Tort Claims Act to allow claims against the U.S. for negligence in military medical care.
Provides for a reduction of claims by the present value of other benefits received.

topic.name: Social Security and Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection and reform of Social Security, Medicare, and related healthcare policies.

Document 1: Amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 to make it out of order... for any fiscal year less than the surplus of the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund
Declares that, if the President's budget recommends an on-budget surplus... then it shall include a detailed proposal for Social Security or Medicare reform legislation.
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to require states to pay dispensing fees for Medicaid covered outpatient drugs.
Directs the Secretary... to collaborate with the Commissioner of Social Security for use of the Commissioner's system for obtaining information held by financial institutions.

topic.name: Public Policy and Social Welfare
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at improving public health, education, housing, cultural property protection, and support for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to authorize the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to make grants and enter contracts to train elementary and secondary school nurses
prepare school nurses to protect and preserve life
Document 2: Amends the Housing Act of 1949 and the Doug Bereuter Section 502 Single Family Housing Loan Guarantee Act
authorize the Secretary to guarantee such loans in aggregate amounts of up to $8 billion for FY2010
Document 3: Amends the Computer Fraud and Abuse Act to set penalties of a fine, imprisonment for at least 25 years
intentionally accessing a computer without authorization or exceeding authorized access
Document 4: ensure that Department of Homeland Security (DHS) components enhance and unify their efforts to interdict, detain, seize, and investigate cultural property
protect cultural property pursuant to obligations under international conventions
Document 5: provides a categorical exclusion under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (NEPA) for a vegetation management project
neither an Environmental Assessment nor an Environmental Impact Statement is required
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to revise the process for reviewing an institution of higher education's compliance
conduct either mandatory or risk-based program reviews
Document 7: Reauthorizes the Special Olympics Sport and Empowerment Act of 2004 for FY2011-FY2015
activities to increase the full participation of people with intellectual disabilities in inclusive school and community activities

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the regulation of various sectors including broadcasting, finance, foreign manufacturing, military conduct, education privacy, presidential authority, and judicial jurisdiction.

Document 1: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to revise the FCC's interpretations of, and enforcement policies concerning, FCC regulations on indecent material
treat as indecent, for purposes of broadcasting during specified hours, any advertisement for medications treating erectile dysfunction or for male enhancement.
Document 2: Prohibits the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) and their affiliates from making lobbying expenditures
Declares that violating this Act shall constitute violation of federal law limiting the use of appropriated funds to influence certain federal contracting and financial transactions.
Document 3: Directs the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to require foreign manufacturers and producers of such products to establish a registered agent in the United States
Subjects an importer to provide a declaration that the foreign manufacturer has registered an agent in the United States.
Document 4: Requires the officials authorized to receive a restricted reporting by a member of the Armed Forces to include judge advocates
Directs the Secretary of Defense to modify the Military Rules of Evidence to provide a member the privilege to refuse to disclose in a military proceeding a confidential communication.
Document 5: Amends provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act of 1974 that prohibit the Department of Education from funding educational agencies
Expands the list of organizations exempt from such prohibitions to include organizations conducting studies for child welfare agencies.
Document 6: Prohibits any state or federal court from relying on or deferring to a presidential signing statement as a source of authority
Requires any federal or state court to permit Congress to participate as an amicus curiae.
Document 7: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions pending in, or on appeal from, the U.S. Court of Federal Claims
Requires the court presiding over the action that was filed later to stay the later action until the action that was filed first is no longer pending.

topic.name: Counterterrorism and National Security
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing U.S. counterterrorism efforts and national security policies.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide training for administering community awareness briefings
incorporate the public testimonials of former extremists into its efforts to combat terrorist recruitment.
Document 2: Prohibits any federal department, agency, or federal government contractor from making a monetary payment to any official of a foreign country for the purposes of bribery, coercion, or any illegal activity
Requires the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA) to report to Congress on all monetary payments made by the CIA to officials of the Government of Afghanistan.
Document 4: authorizes a state or local government to adopt and enforce measures to divest its assets from, or prohibit investment of its assets in: an entity that such government determines, using credible information available to the public, engages in a commerce or investment-related boycott, divestment, or sanctions activity targeting Israel
Such a measure by a state or local government is not preempted by any federal law.

topic.name: Healthcare Access and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving access to healthcare services and regulating various aspects of healthcare delivery.

Document 1: Medicare Patient Access to Physical Therapists Act of 2009
authorize qualified physical therapists to provide services for Medicare beneficiaries without the requirement of a physician referral.
Document 2: Therapeutic Fraud Prevention Act
Prohibits sexual orientation or gender identity conversion therapy from being provided in exchange for monetary compensation.
Document 3: Microbicide Development Act
require the Director of the Office of AIDS Research to expedite the implementation of the federal strategic plan for the conduct and support of microbicide research.
Document 4: Air Ambulance Patient Safety, Protection, and Coordination Act
Authorizes states to regulate medical aspects of intrastate air ambulance services.
Document 6: Medicare Patient Empowerment Act of 2015
allows any Medicare beneficiary to enter into a contract with an eligible professional for any item or service covered by Medicare.

topic.name: Trade and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative measures related to trade, transportation security, and environmental protection.

Document 1: amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
exempt from duty treatment the residue of bulk cargo
Document 2: Includes as renewable energy
hydroelectric energy generated in the state of Alaska
Document 3: establish systems to inspect cargo
ensure the security of all cargo transported in domestic or foreign all-cargo aircraft
Document 4: prohibit the importation or shipment of the species of mussels
covering quagga mussels
Document 5: reauthorizes the gas and hazardous liquid pipeline
pipeline integrity program
Document 6: urgent that the Transportation Worker Identification Credential (TWIC) application process be reformed
deploy TWIC readers as soon as practicable
Document 7: allows the United States to prosecute certain U.S. employees
conduct in Canada that would constitute a federal criminal offense

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving federal oversight and management of regulatory processes, environmental protections, and wildlife conservation efforts.

document_1: establish an advisory committee to review and comment on any proposed change in FAA regulations
affects airport operations, airport capacity, the environment, or communities in the vicinity of airports
document_2: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to issue one-year permits for the lethal taking of California sea lions
nonlethal means of preventing sea lion predation of salmonid stocks is preferable to lethal means
document_3: establish Federal Executive Boards to strengthen the management and administration of agency activities
provide a forum for the exchange of information relating to programs and management methods
document_4: maintain the current leadership rank, aircraft, and core functions of the 354th Fighter Wing
completion of an environmental impact statement and a record of decision relating to the proposed changes
document_5: develop a methodology for conducting a national assessment of the geological storage capacity for carbon dioxide
provide the heads of stakeholder federal agencies with an opportunity to review and comment on the proposed methodology
document_6: expand the reporting requirements of electronic communication and remote computing service providers
encourage foreign governments to combat child sexual exploitation and pornography
document_7: prohibits the head of a federal agency from using an estimate for the social cost of carbon until the completion of the rulemaking notice period
requires an agency head to submit to relevant congressional committees a report on the result of, and methods used to calculate, the cost-benefits or regulatory impact analysis

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments and Programs
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at improving federal programs related to public corruption, health technology, foreign aid, marine conservation, and agricultural disease prevention.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to establish a six-year limitation period for the prosecution of certain public corruption crimes
Authorizes appropriations for additional Department of Justice (DOJ) personnel to investigate and prosecute public corruption offenses.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to establish in the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) the Health Technology Program
serve as a national laboratory and technology development program for global health.
Document 3: Amends the Departments of Commerce, Justice, and State, the Judiciary, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 1993 to extend the alien investor (EB-5) regional center program for five years
to the extent practicable, qualifying program investments should be made in targeted employment areas including rural areas and high unemployment areas.
Document 4: This bill requires the President to establish guidelines for the establishment of measurable goals, performance metrics, and monitoring and evaluation plans for U.S. foreign development and economic assistance
The President shall provide Congress with a detailed description of such guidelines.
Document 5: Amends the Northwest Straits Marine Conservation Initiative Act to define 'Northwest Straits' as the marine waters of the Strait of Juan de Fuca and of Puget Sound
States the goal of the Commission as the protection and restoration of the marine waters, habitats, and species of the Northwest Straits region.
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to delay implementation of Round 2 of the Medicare DMEPOS competitive acquisition program
Requires the Secretary, in the case of any such new competition, to impose a civil monetary penalty of $10,000 on the entity contracted to implement the competitive bidding program.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to prohibit the importation into the United States of any ruminant or swine
until the Secretary certifies to Congress that every region of Argentina is free of foot and mouth disease without vaccination.
